title: Expose session P&L on dashboard — show today's performance separate from lifetime
objective: Surface session P&L (performance since last bot restart) on the dashboard so the operator can distinguish today's trading from lifetime history at a glance. Currently only lifetime realized P&L is shown. The session_start_ts already exists in bot_core.py as an in-memory global — it just needs to be written to state.json and then read and computed by dashboard_server.py.
context: bot_core.py sets _session_start_ts = int(time.time()) at module load. This value is used internally by _session_loss_exceeded() but is never written to state.json or exposed via API. dashboard_server.py reads state.json for all bankroll fields. The fix is two-step: (1) write session_start_ts into state.json in bot_core.py _write_state(), and (2) have dashboard_server.py read that timestamp and compute session_pnl = SUM(pnl_usd WHERE ts_close >= session_start_ts) from the DB, then add session_pnl and session_start_ts to the /api/state bankroll block.
do_not_touch: Any file not listed in allowed_files above

instructions: Read the file. Locate _write_state() — the function that writes runtime/state.json.
Find where the 'pnl' block is assembled inside _write_state().
Do not change any other part of _write_state(). Do not change _session_start_ts initialization.
This is a one-line addition to the state dict. Nothing else changes.
Read the file. Locate the /api/state handler and the bankroll block assembly.
Find where state.json is read and bankroll fields are assembled for the response.

After reading session_start_ts from the state dict (default to 0 if missing), add a DB query: session_pnl = SELECT COALESCE(SUM(pnl_usd), 0) FROM trades WHERE status='closed' AND ts_close >= session_start_ts
Add two new fields to the bankroll block in the /api/state response: 'session_pnl': session_pnl and 'session_start_ts': session_start_ts
If session_start_ts is 0 or missing, session_pnl should return 0 gracefully — do not raise an error.
Also add session_pnl to the /api/bankroll response if that endpoint exists and has a net_pnl field, for consistency.
Use the same DB connection pattern already used in dashboard_server.py — do not introduce a new DB path.

acceptance_criteria: bot_core.py _write_state() writes session_start_ts to state.json pnl block
dashboard_server.py /api/state bankroll block includes session_pnl and session_start_ts
session_pnl = SUM(pnl_usd WHERE closed AND ts_close >= session_start_ts)
session_start_ts missing from state.json returns session_pnl=0 gracefully, no crash
python -m py_compile passes for both files
verification: Read state.json after a bot loop tick and confirm session_start_ts field is present in pnl block
Call GET /api/state and confirm bankroll block contains session_pnl and session_start_ts
Confirm session_pnl is <= net_pnl (session can't exceed lifetime)
Confirm session_pnl changes correctly if a trade closes during the session
rollback: Remove session_start_ts from _write_state() pnl block in bot_core.py. Remove session_pnl and session_start_ts reads from dashboard_server.py. Restart bot.
restart_note: bot_core.py change requires restart to begin writing session_start_ts to state.json.
deliverables: Exact lines changed in both files
Sample /api/state response snippet showing session_pnl and session_start_ts fields
